preamble: The elision of [ε] is a standard historical process of vowel loss, particularly in unstressed suffixes and case endings. Over time, the vowel reduces to a schwa before disappearing entirely.

examples.note: Loss of the dative singular case ending.

sources: Campbell, L. (2013). Historical Linguistics: An Introduction. MIT Press.
Baugh, A. C. & Cable, T. (2013). A History of the English Language (6th ed.). Routledge.
